content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
Novetnber 18, 19 91
John D. Adkisson
Nossanan, Guthner, Knox & Elliott
915 L Street, Suite 1000
Sacranento, CA 95814-3701
Re: 5::"li:"i:: i:5,i8I1"'
Dear Mr. Adkisson:
This is to confirrn the advice lre gave to you in our neeting
on November 14, 1991. In addition to you and ne, the meeting was
attended by conrmission Acting General counsel Scott HaIlabrin,
Commission Executive Director wayne ordos, carla wardlow from the
Conmission's Technical Assistance and Analysis Division and
Margaret catzen of your office. 
The neetinq concerned the duties
of your client, the california water Association, under the
PoliticaI Reforn Act (the rra"1tr1.1
The california water Association is a non-profit organization
of Catifornia private water companies. The Association and its
nembers have estabLished a.progran, entitled ttoperation Gobble,"
in which lrater companies will donate turkeys to various local
charities during the upcorning holiday season. The Association has
contacted California legislators who represent the areas servj'ced
by participating water cornpanies and asked them to identify
pirticular 
charitable groups in their districts to which it would
be appropriate to distribute the turkeys. Furthermore, these
Iegislators may actually attend the events at which the turkeys
are distributed and participate in the events j-n some manner.
Apparently, in years past some of these events have been well-
attended and have gained significant media attention.
1 Go*r"rn."nt Code Sections 81OOo-91015. AI1 statutory references
are to the Governnent Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Conmission
regulations appear at 2 caLifornia Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
oivision 6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 8OO . P.O. Box 8O7 o Sacramento CA 95804.0807 . (916) 322.5660


Our Fl■e No. A-9■-5■2
Page 2
Essentially, you have asked if this activity will result in
the naking of a contribution or a gift to these legislators under
the Act.
The actlvity you describe ls not a Pa)rnent to the legislator
and, therefore, is not a gift under Section 82028. Rather, as we
indicated to you in our neeting, the activity involves a degree of
planning between the water conpany and legislator, and could
iesult eittrer in free publicity to the legislator or in providing
the legislator rrith a political forun. Therefore, depending on
how the event is arranged, any expenditure roade by the water
company in connection with this event could be cLassified as an
in-Xina contribution to the legislator. 
(See Section 82015 and
Regulation 18215 (b) . )
consequently, we set forth for you the conditions under which
we believe these events may be arranged under which no
contribution witl be roade from the vater conpany to the
participating legislator. 
These conditions are as follolrs:
(1) The turkeys will not be delivered to the legislator but
will be delivered by the rater conpanies directly to the fanilies
or the charitable organizations.
(2) In this instance, Legislators nay be consulted for
advice regarding which charitable groups are Eost appropriate in
their distrlcts. 
The rrater conpany roay also coordinate scheduling
of the charitable event with th- legislator, but witl otherwise
organize the event ulthout particlpation of the legislator. . the
ev6nt wilt not be an exclusive polltical forum for any particular
elected offlcial, but shaII be open to the public lncluding any
potential political opponents of the elected official.
(3) Any press releases naning the elected official shall be
sent by the legislator without participation by the water conpany.
Any pr-ess releise sent by the water conpany or the Association
shall not identify a particular elected official by nane, but may
indicate that local legislators are expected to attend' At the
event, no posters or signs or labels on turkeys bearing the narues
of elected officiaLs shall be made part of the event by the lrater
company. It is recognized that the lrater conpany cannot restrain
ereitei officials or Denbers of the public froro wearing T-shirts,
buttons, or bringing other political roaterial, but the event
itself wiIl not deliberately feature any such publicity'
(4) No representation will be nade that the source of the
donation is the elected official. 
On the other hand, it may be
cornrnunicated to the charity that an elected official designated
the charity. The donation itself will clearly be credited to the
water conpany.


Our Fi■e No. A-9■-5■2
Page 3
(5) No costs associated wlth the charitable event shall be
reportable so long as the costs are for incidental natters such as
refreshments for volunteers, press releases, transPortation costs,
or any costs necessary to deliver the turkeys. on the other hand,
any signif j.cant costs aiDed at creating a political forum for an
elected official r,riIl be examined on a case-by-case basis. For
example, if a water company rents a hall or buys television. tirne
to feature a presentation by an elected officiaL in connection
with the event, a political contribution might result.
Even under the conditions described above, there exists the
posslbitity that legislators will receive political publicity and
tenefits as a result of expenditures by participating vrater
conpanies. However, given the inninent onset of the hotiday
season and the close legal call posed by this activity the
connission does not desire to disrupt such an obviously vrorthwhile
charitable progran at this late date. Therefore, under the
conditions stated above and for the 1991 holiday season only, the
Conrnission wiII not require the reporting of a contribution.
After this year, if the Association desires to avoid having its
meDbers nake a reportable contribution, it should restructure the
proerram to avoid consultation between the participating water
Loupanies and the legislators in regards to the donation of the
turkeys and related publlc events. specifically, it would be
bettei if the water conpanles designated their own charities
rather than relying on the legislators to do so. Further, the
speclfic events at whlch the turkeys are given away should be
slheduled without first consutting with the legislators' office.
If you have any guestions, please contact scott Haflabrin at
(916) 322-5901.
S incere Iy ,
SH:BD:aa
en Davidian
―_

sections.question: Essentially, you have asked if this activity will result in the making of a contribution or a gift to these legislators under the Act.
sections.conclusion: The activity you describe is not a Payment to the legislator and, therefore, is not a gift under Section 82028. Rather, as we indicated to you in our meeting, the activity involves a degree of planning between the water company and legislator, and could result either in free publicity to the legislator or in providing the legislator with a political forum. Therefore, depending on how the event is arranged, any expenditure made by the water company in connection with this event could be classified as an in-kind contribution to the legislator.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether the California Water Association's 'Operation Gobble' program, in which water companies donate turkeys to charities with legislator involvement in charity selection and event attendance, constitutes a reportable gift or contribution under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concluded that while the activity could constitute an in-kind contribution depending on arrangement, it provided five specific conditions under which no contribution would be reportable for the 1991 holiday season only. After 1991, the Association must restructure the program to eliminate legislator consultation and involvement to avoid reportable contributions.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is well-structured FPPC advice letter dated November 18, 1991. Question and conclusion sections identified with clear synthetic summaries. Five conditional requirements clearly enumerated. Minor OCR errors noted in letterhead and name spelling (e.g., 'Novetnber' for November, 'Guthner' likely Guthner) but do not affect content extraction.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Essentially, you have asked if this activity will result in the making of a contribution or a gift to these legislators under the Act.

CONCLUSION: The activity you describe is not a Payment to the legislator and, therefore, is not a gift under Section 82028. Rather, as we indicated to you in our meeting, the activity involves a degree of planning between the water company and legislator, and could result either in free publicity to the legislator or in providing the legislator with a political forum. Therefore, depending on how the event is arranged, any expenditure made by the water company in connection with this event could be classified as an in-kind contribution to the legislator.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advised the California Water Association on compliance with the Political Reform Act regarding its 'Operation Gobble' turkey donation program involving legislator participation. The Commission provided conditional approval for 1991 with five specific conditions to avoid reportable contributions, but required restructuring in future years.
